claim: AP_GPS::all_consistent considers two GPS receivers consistent when the distance between their reported positions is less than 50m.
label: supported
notes: Chunk explicitly returns true (consistent) for a single receiver and uses distance < 50m; the unsupported claim inverts the single-receiver behavior while reusing 'one valid receiver' / 'return'.

claim: AP_GPS::all_consistent returns false when only one valid GPS receiver is present.
label: unsupported
notes: Chunk explicitly returns true (consistent) for a single receiver and uses distance < 50m; the unsupported claim inverts the single-receiver behavior while reusing 'one valid receiver' / 'return'.

claim: AC_AttitudeControl::lean_angle_max_cd multiplies the constrained angle-max value by 100 to convert it to centidegrees.
label: supported
notes: Code multiplies by 100, not divides; unsupported claim reuses the exact MIN/MAX identifiers and the number 100 but flips the operation.

claim: AC_AttitudeControl::lean_angle_max_cd constrains the angle-max value between AC_ATTITUDE_CONTROL_ANGLE_LIMIT_MIN and AC_ATTITUDE_CONTROL_ANGLE_LIMIT_MAX and then divides the result by 100.
label: unsupported
notes: Code multiplies by 100, not divides; unsupported claim reuses the exact MIN/MAX identifiers and the number 100 but flips the operation.

claim: AP_MotorsMulticopter::check_mot_pwm_params returns false when _pwm_min is less than 1 or _pwm_min is greater than or equal to _pwm_max.
label: supported
notes: Real condition checks _pwm_min<1 and _pwm_min>=_pwm_max; unsupported reuses _pwm_min/_pwm_max and the constant 1 but states a fabricated condition on _pwm_max.

claim: AP_MotorsMulticopter::check_mot_pwm_params returns false when _pwm_max is less than 1 or when _pwm_max equals _pwm_min plus one.
label: unsupported
notes: Real condition checks _pwm_min<1 and _pwm_min>=_pwm_max; unsupported reuses _pwm_min/_pwm_max and the constant 1 but states a fabricated condition on _pwm_max.

claim: MissionItemProtocol_Fence::get_item returns MAV_MISSION_INVALID_SEQUENCE when the requested seq is greater than or equal to the number of stored fence items.
label: supported
notes: On get_item_as_mission_item failure the code returns MAV_MISSION_ERROR, not MAV_MISSION_INVALID_SEQUENCE; unsupported swaps the two return codes that both appear in the chunk.

claim: MissionItemProtocol_Fence::get_item returns MAV_MISSION_INVALID_SEQUENCE when get_item_as_mission_item fails to populate the packet.
label: unsupported
notes: On get_item_as_mission_item failure the code returns MAV_MISSION_ERROR, not MAV_MISSION_INVALID_SEQUENCE; unsupported swaps the two return codes that both appear in the chunk.

claim: AP_InertialSensor_Backend::sensors_converging returns true only while the board uptime is below HAL_INS_CONVERGANCE_MS and the vehicle is not soft-armed.
label: supported
notes: Condition is millis64() < HAL_INS_CONVERGANCE_MS && !get_soft_armed(); unsupported reuses the identifier but inverts both the time comparison and the armed predicate.

claim: AP_InertialSensor_Backend::sensors_converging returns true once the board uptime exceeds HAL_INS_CONVERGANCE_MS as long as the vehicle is soft-armed.
label: unsupported
notes: Condition is millis64() < HAL_INS_CONVERGANCE_MS && !get_soft_armed(); unsupported reuses the identifier but inverts both the time comparison and the armed predicate.

chunk_text:
Glitch Protection - Default
===========================

GPS glitch protection is provided which can help alert the
pilot to a bad GPS position, trigger a failsafe and reduce the incidents
of fly-aways.  Glitches are detected by comparing the each new position
update received from the GPS with a position projected out from the
previous update's position and velocity.

.. image:: ../images/GPSGlitchDetection.jpg
    :target: ../_images/GPSGlitchDetection.jpg

The new position is accepted as "good" if:

#. the two positions are within the ``GPSGLITCH_RADIUS`` (defaults to
   5m).
#. the new position is within a radius that is 10m/s/s
   (``GPSGLITCH_ACCEL``) \* dt \* dt.  Where "dt" is the time difference
   between the two samples.

During a glitch, "Bad Position" will be written on the ground station's
HUD
claim: A new GPS position is accepted as good if it is within the GPSGLITCH_RADIUS, which defaults to 5m.
label: supported
notes: GPSGLITCH_RADIUS defaults to 5m; GPSGLITCH_ACCEL is an acceleration (10 m/s/s) not a 10m radius. Unsupported reuses both identifiers and the number 10 but conflates accel with the radius default.

chunk_text:
Glitch Protection - Default
===========================

GPS glitch protection is provided which can help alert the
pilot to a bad GPS position, trigger a failsafe and reduce the incidents
of fly-aways.  Glitches are detected by comparing the each new position
update received from the GPS with a position projected out from the
previous update's position and velocity.

.. image:: ../images/GPSGlitchDetection.jpg
    :target: ../_images/GPSGlitchDetection.jpg

The new position is accepted as "good" if:

#. the two positions are within the ``GPSGLITCH_RADIUS`` (defaults to
   5m).
#. the new position is within a radius that is 10m/s/s
   (``GPSGLITCH_ACCEL``) \* dt \* dt.  Where "dt" is the time difference
   between the two samples.

During a glitch, "Bad Position" will be written on the ground station's
HUD
claim: GPS glitch protection accepts a new position only if it is within GPSGLITCH_ACCEL, which defaults to 10m.
label: unsupported
notes: GPSGLITCH_RADIUS defaults to 5m; GPSGLITCH_ACCEL is an acceleration (10 m/s/s) not a 10m radius. Unsupported reuses both identifiers and the number 10 but conflates accel with the radius default.

chunk_text:
When will it trigger?
=====================

The EKF failsafe will trigger when any two of the EKF "variances" for compass, position or velocity are higher than the :ref:`FS_EKF_THRESH <FS_EKF_THRESH>` parameter value for 1 second.

These "variances" come from the EKF itself and are numbers indicating the EKF's confidence in its estimates.  The values are between 0 and 1 with 0 meaning the estimate is very trustworthy and 1.0 is very untrustworthy.

The EKF calculates these "variances" by comparing the results from multiple sensors.  So for example, if the GPS position suddenly jumps but the accelerometers do not show a sudden acceleration, the EKF variance for position would climb (i.e become less trustworthy)

The variances can be viewed in real-time on the ground station.  If using Mission Planner click on the "EKF" label on the HUD.

.. image:: ../images/ekf-failsafe-variance-viewer.png
    :target: ../_images/ekf-failsafe-variance-viewer.png
    :width: 450px
claim: The EKF failsafe triggers when any two of the EKF variances for compass, position or velocity exceed the FS_EKF_THRESH value for 1 second.
label: supported
notes: Text says any TWO variances ABOVE the threshold; unsupported reuses FS_EKF_THRESH and '1 second' but changes 'two'->'single' and 'higher than'->'below'.

chunk_text:
When will it trigger?
=====================

The EKF failsafe will trigger when any two of the EKF "variances" for compass, position or velocity are higher than the :ref:`FS_EKF_THRESH <FS_EKF_THRESH>` parameter value for 1 second.

These "variances" come from the EKF itself and are numbers indicating the EKF's confidence in its estimates.  The values are between 0 and 1 with 0 meaning the estimate is very trustworthy and 1.0 is very untrustworthy.

The EKF calculates these "variances" by comparing the results from multiple sensors.  So for example, if the GPS position suddenly jumps but the accelerometers do not show a sudden acceleration, the EKF variance for position would climb (i.e become less trustworthy)

The variances can be viewed in real-time on the ground station.  If using Mission Planner click on the "EKF" label on the HUD.

.. image:: ../images/ekf-failsafe-variance-viewer.png
    :target: ../_images/ekf-failsafe-variance-viewer.png
    :width: 450px
claim: The EKF failsafe triggers when any single EKF variance for compass, position or velocity stays below the FS_EKF_THRESH value for 1 second.
label: unsupported
notes: Text says any TWO variances ABOVE the threshold; unsupported reuses FS_EKF_THRESH and '1 second' but changes 'two'->'single' and 'higher than'->'below'.

chunk_text:
Adequate Power
==============

It is very important that the vehicle has enough power available. 
Without this the altitude hold and attitude controllers can require more power
than is available from one or more motors and will be forced to
sacrifice some control which could lead to a loss of attitude or
altitude.

Ideally the vehicle should be able to hover at about 50% throttle (mid
stick) and anything higher than 70% is dangerous.

.. warning::

   If you incorporate expo on your transmitter, that directly
   increases the effective size of the throttle dead band.
claim: The vehicle should ideally be able to hover at about 50% throttle, and anything higher than 70% throttle is considered dangerous.
label: supported
notes: Doc: hover ~50%, >70% dangerous; unsupported swaps the two numbers and the danger direction while keeping 50/70.

chunk_text:
Adequate Power
==============

It is very important that the vehicle has enough power available. 
Without this the altitude hold and attitude controllers can require more power
than is available from one or more motors and will be forced to
sacrifice some control which could lead to a loss of attitude or
altitude.

Ideally the vehicle should be able to hover at about 50% throttle (mid
stick) and anything higher than 70% is dangerous.

.. warning::

   If you incorporate expo on your transmitter, that directly
   increases the effective size of the throttle dead band.
claim: The document recommends that the vehicle hover at about 70% throttle, and states that anything below 50% throttle is dangerous.
label: unsupported
notes: Doc: hover ~50%, >70% dangerous; unsupported swaps the two numbers and the danger direction while keeping 50/70.

chunk_text:
Compass Calibration
===================

You need to calibrate the compass in your Disco before you fly. This
must be done with the hatch in place due to the magnetic catch on the
hatch.

Each GCS choice has an option to start a compass calibration. Please
choose on-board compass calibration for your GCS and follow the
prompts.

Note that you may find you need to raise COMPASS_CAL_FIT to allow
successful calibration of the Disco, as the magnetic setup of the
Disco hardware is not ideal and won't produce a perfect fit. We
recommend setting COMPASS_CAL_FIT to 20.
claim: For the Disco airframe it is recommended to set COMPASS_CAL_FIT to 20 to allow successful compass calibration.
label: supported
notes: Doc says RAISE COMPASS_CAL_FIT to 20 to allow a successful fit; it does not claim this reduces hatch magnetic interference. Unsupported reuses 'COMPASS_CAL_FIT','20','magnetic catch'/'hatch' with a fabricated causal claim.

chunk_text:
Compass Calibration
===================

You need to calibrate the compass in your Disco before you fly. This
must be done with the hatch in place due to the magnetic catch on the
hatch.

Each GCS choice has an option to start a compass calibration. Please
choose on-board compass calibration for your GCS and follow the
prompts.

Note that you may find you need to raise COMPASS_CAL_FIT to allow
successful calibration of the Disco, as the magnetic setup of the
Disco hardware is not ideal and won't produce a perfect fit. We
recommend setting COMPASS_CAL_FIT to 20.
claim: For the Disco airframe, lowering COMPASS_CAL_FIT to 20 reduces the magnetic interference produced by the hatch's magnetic catch.
label: unsupported
notes: Doc says RAISE COMPASS_CAL_FIT to 20 to allow a successful fit; it does not claim this reduces hatch magnetic interference. Unsupported reuses 'COMPASS_CAL_FIT','20','magnetic catch'/'hatch' with a fabricated causal claim.

chunk_text:
Parameter: SAIL_ANGLE_IDEAL (leaf ANGLE_IDEAL)
Vehicle: rover
Group prefix: SAIL_
Display name: Sail ideal angle
Description: Ideal angle between sail and apparent wind
Units: deg
Range: 0 to 90
Default: 25
claim: The SAIL_ANGLE_IDEAL parameter has a default of 25 degrees and a valid range of 0 to 90 degrees.
label: supported
notes: Default is 25 (range 0-90); unsupported reuses range bound 90 as the default and changes 'ideal angle' to 'maximum angle'.

chunk_text:
Parameter: SAIL_ANGLE_IDEAL (leaf ANGLE_IDEAL)
Vehicle: rover
Group prefix: SAIL_
Display name: Sail ideal angle
Description: Ideal angle between sail and apparent wind
Units: deg
Range: 0 to 90
Default: 25
claim: The SAIL_ANGLE_IDEAL parameter has a default of 90 degrees and represents the maximum angle between the sail and the apparent wind.
label: unsupported
notes: Default is 25 (range 0-90); unsupported reuses range bound 90 as the default and changes 'ideal angle' to 'maximum angle'.
